title: Subcircuit assigns needles to haystack nodes already in use during extract pass
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.22+29 (git sha1 fc53a0a5c, gcc 11.2.0-19ubuntu1 -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

There exists a problem with the `extract` pass. If a map is passed that contains a module that implements multiple gates of the same type, the sub circuit resolver assigns a needle node to a haystack node, that has been assigned to a different needle node. 

There exists a provision against this type of problem, but this works cross  ullman recursion. This problem occurs within a recursion.

Relevant output:
```
7.5. Running solver from SubCircuit library.
Solving for needle_mod_74x08_3 in haystack_mod_and_or.

Needle nodes:
    0: $and$./minimal.v:13$9 ($and)
    1: $and$./minimal.v:14$10 ($and)
    2: $and$./minimal.v:15$11 ($and)

Haystack nodes:
    0: $and$./and_or.v:10$2 ($and)
    1: $and$./and_or.v:10$3 ($and)
    2: $or$./and_or.v:10$4 ($or)

Needle Adjecency Matrix:
          0:   1:   2:
    0:    -    -    -
    1:    -    -    -
    2:    -    -    -

Haystack Adjecency Matrix:
          0:   1:   2:
    0:    -    -    0
    1:    -    -    1
    2:    2    3    -

Edge Types:
    0: $and(\A[1],\B[1],\Y[1]) $or(\A[1],\B[1],\Y[1]) \Y[0]:\A[0]
    1: $and(\A[1],\B[1],\Y[1]) $or(\A[1],\B[1],\Y[1]) \Y[0]:\B[0]
    2: $or(\A[1],\B[1],\Y[1]) $and(\A[1],\B[1],\Y[1]) \A[0]:\Y[0]
    3: $or(\A[1],\B[1],\Y[1]) $and(\A[1],\B[1],\Y[1]) \B[0]:\Y[0]

Enumeration Matrix (haystack nodes at column indices):
       0     
    0: **.
    1: **.
    2: **.

Enumeration Matrix at recursion level 0 (0):
       0     
    0: **.
    1: **.
    2: **.

Portmapper results:
    0: $and$./minimal.v:13$9
       variant  0: \A -> \A, \B -> \B, \Y -> \Y
    1: $and$./minimal.v:14$10
       variant  0: \A -> \A, \B -> \B, \Y -> \Y
    2: $and$./minimal.v:15$11
       variant  0: \A -> \A, \B -> \B, \Y -> \Y

Solution:
       0     
    0: *..
    1: .*.
    2: .*.
Solving for needle_mod_74x32_1 in haystack_mod_and_or.

Needle nodes:
    0: $or$./minimal.v:25$12 ($or)

Haystack nodes:
    0: $and$./and_or.v:10$2 ($and)
    1: $and$./and_or.v:10$3 ($and)
    2: $or$./and_or.v:10$4 ($or)

Needle Adjecency Matrix:
          0:
    0:    -

Haystack Adjecency Matrix:
          0:   1:   2:
    0:    -    -    0
    1:    -    -    1
    2:    2    3    -

Edge Types:
    0: $and(\A[1],\B[1],\Y[1]) $or(\A[1],\B[1],\Y[1]) \Y[0]:\A[0]
    1: $and(\A[1],\B[1],\Y[1]) $or(\A[1],\B[1],\Y[1]) \Y[0]:\B[0]
    2: $or(\A[1],\B[1],\Y[1]) $and(\A[1],\B[1],\Y[1]) \A[0]:\Y[0]
    3: $or(\A[1],\B[1],\Y[1]) $and(\A[1],\B[1],\Y[1]) \B[0]:\Y[0]

Enumeration Matrix (haystack nodes at column indices):
       0     
    0: ..*

Portmapper results:
    0: $or$./minimal.v:25$12
       variant  0: \A -> \A, \B -> \B, \Y -> \Y

Solution:
       0     
    0: ..*
Found 2 matches.

7.6. Substitute SubCircuits with cells.

Match #0: (needle_mod_74x08_3 in haystack_mod_and_or)
  $and$./minimal.v:13$9 -> $and$./and_or.v:10$2 \A:\A \B:\B \Y:\Y
  $and$./minimal.v:14$10 -> $and$./and_or.v:10$3 \A:\A \B:\B \Y:\Y
  $and$./minimal.v:15$11 -> $and$./and_or.v:10$3 \A:\A \B:\B \Y:\Y
terminate called after throwing an instance of 'std::out_of_range'
  what():  dict::at()
Aborted (core dumped)
```

YoSys regard this following as a valid solution
```
Solution:
       0     
    0: *..
    1: .*.
    2: .*.
```

Here, needle 1(row) is assigned to haystack1(column)
Then, needle 2 is also assigned to haystack1

This results in the following part of the output:
```
$and$./minimal.v:14$10 -> $and$./and_or.v:10$3 \A:\A \B:\B \Y:\Y
$and$./minimal.v:15$11 -> $and$./and_or.v:10$3 \A:\A \B:\B \Y:\Y
```

Note that `$10` and `$11` are both assigned to `$3`
At some point, after processing, something gets deleted, and then accessed again causing the exception, but that's a symptom rather than cause.

To reproduce
`minimal.v`
```
// Tripe AND GATE
module mod_74x08_3 (
    input A_1,
    input B_1,
    input A_2,
    input B_2,
    input A_3,
    input B_3,
    output Y_1,
    output Y_2,
    output Y_3);

assign Y_1 = A_1 & B_1;
assign Y_2 = A_2 & B_2;
assign Y_3 = A_3 & B_3;

endmodule

// OR GATE
module mod_74x32_1 (
    input A_1,
    input B_1,
    output Y_1);

assign Y_1 = A_1 | B_1;
endmodule
```

`and_or.v`
```
module mod_and_or (
    input a,
    input b,
    input c,
    input d,
    output reg y
);

always @(a,b,c,d) begin
    y <= (a&b)|(c&d);
end
endmodule

```

`script.ys`
```
read_verilog ./and_or.v
hierarchy -top mod_and_or
proc
opt
techmap -map ./minimal.v
proc
extract -map ./minimal.v -verbose
proc
```

Lump everthing in one dir and execute:
`yosys -s script.ys`





### Expected Behavior

Substitution to fail, since there is no valid sub circuit present that can be substituted with.

This without throwing an exception

### Actual Behavior

Yosys crashes due to described problem
code_blocks:
module mod_and_or (
    input a,
    input b,
    input c,
    input d,
    output reg y
);

always @(a,b,c,d) begin
    y <= (a&b)|(c&d);
end
endmodule
